# feat(nexus): migrate A2A service from raw FastAPI to Nexus (#445)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `f4359b87ee8d` — feat(nexus): migrate A2A service from raw FastAPI to Nexus (#445)

**Body**:

The A2A HTTP service was creating its own FastAPI() app with manual
CORS, route registration, and Header() extraction. This is the first
application-layer file migrated to Nexus (Track 3 of #445).

Changes:
1. Add Starlette type re-exports to nexus package (Request, Response,
   JSONResponse, StreamingResponse, HTTPException, WebSocket,
   WebSocketDisconnect) so Nexus consumers can import types from
   nexus instead of raw starlette/fastapi
2. Rewrite A2AService.create_app() to use Nexus() with @app.endpoint()
3. Replace Header() dependency injection with direct request.headers
   access — simpler and doesn't require FastAPI DI
4. CORS now configured via Nexus constructor params
5. Health route moved to /a2a/health to avoid conflict with Nexus
   gateway's built-in /health endpoint
6. create_app() returns nexus.fastapi_app for backward compat with
   TestClient and uvicorn

Pre-commit auto-stash workaround — see prior commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
